Page.Width: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualWidth, FallbackValue=700}
Page.Height: {Binding RelativeSource={RelativeSource Mode=FindAncestor, AncestorType={x:Type ContentControl}}, Path=ActualHeight, FallbackValue=700}

Run.Text: Sonobuoys are small, air-deployable floating buoys, which deploy a long line of transducers (microphones) underwater to a preset depth. These are then connected to the helicopter (or aircraft) using a radio link. Note: you must have a helicopter within 30nm of each buoy to receive data from it!

The HSS-2B carries both passive (AN/SSQ-53 DIFAR) and active (AN/SSQ-62 DICASS) sonobuoys. As with shipboard sonar sets, passive will listen only and active will ping for direction and ranging. Note: active sonobuoys are designed to be used to localize and 'fix' a passive contact, and are not designed for searching an area!
